content: First-line psychotherapy: Exposure and Response Prevention (ERP), a form of CBT involving gradual exposure to anxiety-provoking stimuli while preventing compulsive responses. ERP shows durable benefits post-treatment. First-line pharmacotherapy: SSRIs at higher doses than for depression (e.g., fluoxetine 60-80mg, sertraline 150-200mg, fluvoxamine 200-300mg) with adequate trial duration (10-12 weeks). Clomipramine (TCA) is effective but limited by side effects. Augmentation strategies for partial response include low-dose antipsychotics (risperidone, aripiprazole). Treatment typically continues 1-2 years before considering tapering. Deep brain stimulation reserved for severe treatment-refractory cases.
